description_long: This is a compact, futuristic airplane with a sleek, angular design and a metallic blue-gray exterior. The wings are sharply swept back and angled upward, giving the aircraft a dynamic, aggressive profile. The cockpit is centrally located and features a gold-tinted canopy, suggesting a single-seat, enclosed configuration. Twin jet engines are mounted on either side of the fuselage, with prominent exhausts visible at the rear. The tail section is minimalistic, with no traditional vertical stabilizer, and the landing gear appears to be fixed and robust. The overall appearance is reminiscent of a stealth or advanced urban air mobility vehicle, designed for agility and speed.
description: A sleek, angular metallic blue-gray airplane with swept-up wings, gold-tinted canopy, and twin side-mounted jet engines. Its compact, futuristic design features a single-seat cockpit and minimalistic tail.
description_view_0: Front view shows the sharply swept, upward-angled wings, central gold-tinted canopy, and twin jet intakes flanking the fuselage.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlights the full wingspan, central cockpit, and the compact, streamlined fuselage with visible engine housings.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the twin jet exhausts, upward-angled wings, and the minimalistic tail section with no vertical stabilizer.
description_view_3: Bottom view displays the fixed landing gear, underside of the wings, and the streamlined undercarriage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with sharply swept, upward-angled wings and pronounced anhedral angle; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted on either side of the rear fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Minimalistic tail with no visible vertical stabilizer, resembling a tailless or flying wing configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, single-seat, enclosed cockpit with a gold-tinted canopy.
